title: Getting started with Rtractor
text: Rtractor complexity/statistical-physics layer Circadia Lab R ecosystem: shared home nonlinear dynamics complex-systems measures (entropy, fractal dimension, multifractal spectra, recurrence quantification) otherwise get reimplemented piecemeal inside signal-specific packages like mrpheus, zeitR, dynR. Like rest ecosystem, Rtractor signal-agnostic: every function accepts plain numeric vector regardless came – EEG, actigraphy, BOLD, HRV, anything else – rather assuming specific acquisition modality staging scheme. solid reference implementation exists, Rtractor wraps (via Rcpp) rather re-deriving algorithm scratch, preserve numerical parity original methods literature. license permits direct wrap, functions clean-room reimplementations published algorithm, validated reference implementation synthetic test data. See inst/COPYRIGHTS full provenance every function. article walks everything currently implemented, organised family. See “isn’t yet” end ’s still progress.

title: Getting started with Rtractor
text: couple synthetic series work throughout: white noise (uncorrelated signal, classic complexity-measures benchmark) random walk (cumulative sum, classic benchmark).

title: Getting started with Rtractor
text: dfa() estimates scaling exponent alpha time series (Peng et al. 1994). default treats x increment series integrates internally – standard DFA convention – white noise gives textbook benchmark alpha ~ 0.5: Feeding random walk default pipeline amounts double integration – classic benchmark, alpha ~ 1.5:

title: Getting started with Rtractor
text: higuchi_fd() estimates fractal dimension curve length increasing sub-sampling intervals (Higuchi 1988). White noise space-filling (HFD ~ 2); smooth periodic signal line-like (HFD ~ 1):
code: higuchi_fd(white_noise, k_max = 10)$hfd #> [1] 2.000681  smooth_signal <- sin(seq(0, 40 * pi, length.out = 4000)) higuchi_fd(smooth_signal, k_max = 10)$hfd #> [1] 1.002216

title: Getting started with Rtractor
text: mfdma() extends DFA spectrum scaling exponents across multifractal orders q (Gu & Zhou 2010), returning singularity spectrum f(alpha):  White noise close monofractal, spectrum collapses narrow range around alpha ~ 0.5 f(alpha) peaking near 1.
code: mf <- mfdma(white_noise, n_min = 10, n_max = 400, n_scales = 20) plot(mf$alpha, mf$f, type = "b", xlab = "alpha", ylab = "f(alpha)")

title: Getting started with Rtractor
text: chhabra_jensen() estimates kind spectrum via direct box-counting (Chhabra & Jensen 1989) rather detrended fluctuations. needs strictly positive series dyadic (power--two-friendly) length:  q value’s alpha/falpha/Dq estimate comes R-squared (r_squared_alpha, r_squared_falpha, r_squared_Dq) – worth checking trusting individual point, especially near edges q range.
code: positive_series <- abs(rnorm(1024)) + 0.01 cj <- chhabra_jensen(positive_series, scales = 1:6) plot(cj$alpha, cj$falpha, type = "b", xlab = "alpha", ylab = "f(alpha)")

title: Getting started with Rtractor
text: Three fast, cheap--compute descriptors centralised mrpheus’s AASM staging feature pipeline (validated port antropy/YASA Python feature set): petrosian_fd() fast proxy irregularity based sign changes first difference. hjorth_parameters() returns mobility (proxy mean frequency) complexity (proxy bandwidth) variance ratios successive differences – note uses Bessel-corrected variance, matching R’s var() convention, differs slightly antropy’s population-variance convention short series (see ?hjorth_parameters). num_zerocross() simply counts sign changes.

title: Getting started with Rtractor
text: perm_entropy() estimates complexity distribution ordinal patterns series (Bandt & Pompe 2002), normalised [0, 1] default: White noise near-maximal permutation entropy (every ordinal pattern close equally likely); smooth periodic signal much lower entropy (small number patterns dominate).

title: Getting started with Rtractor
text: recurrence_microstate_entropy() implements parameter-free approach recurrence analysis (Corso et al. 2018): rather picking vicinity threshold epsilon hand, searches threshold maximises Shannon entropy recurrence microstate distribution.

title: Getting started with Rtractor
text: Rtractor ships colour palette ggplot2 theme, visually distinct circadia’s (softer, pastel) figures package recognisable glance:
code: rtractor_palette() #>      coral      cream       sage steel_blue        ink  #>  "#FFB6A6"  "#FFEBD3"  "#9BCEC1"  "#67A2C5"  "#23475C" rtractor_palette("core") #>      coral      cream       sage steel_blue  #>  "#FFB6A6"  "#FFEBD3"  "#9BCEC1"  "#67A2C5" library(ggplot2)  mf_df <- data.frame(alpha = mf$alpha, f = mf$f)  ggplot(mf_df, aes(alpha, f)) +   geom_point(colour = rtractor_palette("core")[["steel_blue"]], size = 2) +   geom_line(colour = rtractor_palette("core")[["steel_blue"]]) +   labs(     title = "MFDMA singularity spectrum",     subtitle = "White noise: close to monofractal",     x = expression(alpha), y = expression(f(alpha))   ) +   theme_rtractor()

title: Getting started with Rtractor
text: Several planned families aren’t implemented yet: Lyapunov exponents (R/lyapunov.R) – Rosenstein Wolf methods largest Lyapunov exponent. Multiscale metrics (R/multiscale.R) – multiscale entropy refined composite multiscale entropy, built entropy family applied coarse-grained scale. General RQA measures (R/rqa.R) – recurrence matrix derived quantifiers (determinism, laminarity, recurrence rate, trapping time). recurrence_microstate_entropy() threshold-selection tool, replacement . Phase-space embedding (R/embed.R) – time-delay embedding, delay/dimension estimation, needed Lyapunov RQA families . See NEWS.md progress, package’s GitHub repository current status reference code .

text: Lucas França. Author, maintainer. Mario Leocadio-Miguel. Author.

what: Rtractor — Complexity and nonlinear time series analysis for R
title: Rtractor — Complexity and nonlinear time series analysis for R
text: Complexity nonlinear time series analysis physiological signals — entropy, fractal/multifractal measures, Lyapunov exponents, multiscale metrics, recurrence quantification analysis, wrapping established C/C++/Fortran reference implementations.

title: Rtractor — Complexity and nonlinear time series analysis for R
text: Rtractor shared “complexity toolkit” Circadia Lab / CoDe-Neuro Lab ecosystem: single home nonlinear dynamics complex-systems measures (entropy, fractal dimension, Lyapunov exponents, multiscale entropy, recurrence quantification) otherwise get reimplemented piecemeal inside signal-specific packages like mrpheus, zeitR, dynR. Like hypnoR, Rtractor signal-agnostic: every metric accepts plain numeric time series regardless came — EEG, actigraphy, BOLD, HRV, anything else — rather assuming specific data source staging scheme. possible, Rtractor wraps existing, well-validated C/C++/Fortran reference implementations via Rcpp rather reimplementing algorithms scratch R, preserve numerical parity original methods literature.

title: Rtractor — Complexity and nonlinear time series analysis for R
text: 🌀 Entropy — sample entropy, approximate entropy, permutation entropy 🌿 Fractal & multifractal — Higuchi dimension, box-counting, DFA, MFDFA 🦋 Lyapunov exponents — Rosenstein Wolf methods 🔁 Multiscale metrics — multiscale entropy, refined composite MSE 🕸️ Recurrence quantification analysis (RQA) — recurrence plots, determinism, laminarity, related measures 📐 Phase-space embedding — shared reconstruction utilities (embedding dimension, time delay) underlying Lyapunov RQA families 🎨 dedicated Rtractor colour palette, ggplot2 scales, theme_rtractor() None Lyapunov multiscale metric functions implemented yet. Currently working: dfa(), higuchi_fd(), mfdma(), chhabra_jensen(), petrosian_fd(), hjorth_parameters(), num_zerocross() (fractal family), perm_entropy() (entropy family), recurrence_microstate_entropy() (RQA family). See NEWS.md progress.

title: Rtractor — Complexity and nonlinear time series analysis for R
code: Rtractor/ ├── R/ │   ├── Rtractor-package.R   # package-level doc / Rcpp registration │   ├── entropy.R            # perm_entropy(); planned: sample/approx entropy │   ├── fractal.R            # dfa(), higuchi_fd(), mfdma(), chhabra_jensen(), │   │                       # petrosian_fd(), hjorth_parameters(), num_zerocross() │   ├── lyapunov.R           # planned: Rosenstein/Wolf │   ├── multiscale.R         # planned: MSE, RCMSE │   ├── rqa.R                # recurrence_microstate_entropy(); planned: RQA measures │   ├── embed.R              # planned: phase-space reconstruction utils │   ├── palettes.R           # rtractor_palette(), rtractor_palettes() │   ├── scales.R             # scale_{colour,fill}_rtractor(_c)() │   └── theme.R              # theme_rtractor() ├── src/ │   ├── dfa.cpp              # DFA — wraps PhysioNet's dfa.c (GPL-2+) │   ├── higuchi.cpp          # Higuchi FD — clean-room reimplementation │   ├── fractal_multifractal.cpp  # MFDMA, Chhabra-Jensen — clean-room │   ├── fractal_nonlinear.cpp     # Petrosian FD, Hjorth, zero-crossings — from mrpheus │   ├── entropy.cpp          # Permutation entropy — from mrpheus │   └── microstates.cpp      # Recurrence microstates entropy — wraps MIT code ├── inst/ │   └── COPYRIGHTS           # attribution for wrapped/ported reference code ├── tests/testthat/ ├── man/ ├── DESCRIPTION └── NEWS.md

title: Rtractor — Complexity and nonlinear time series analysis for R

title: Rtractor — Complexity and nonlinear time series analysis for R
code: # once published on r-universe: install.packages("Rtractor", repos = c(   "https://circadia-bio.r-universe.dev",   "https://cloud.r-project.org" ))  # or directly from GitHub: remotes::install_github("circadia-bio/Rtractor")

title: Rtractor — Complexity and nonlinear time series analysis for R
text: Signal-agnostic — every function operates plain numeric vector (matrix, multivariate/embedded methods); assumptions acquisition modality. Isolation principle — Rtractor runs standalone dependency Circadia Lab / CoDe-Neuro Lab package, can adopted independently ecosystem packages leaf dependency. Wrap, don’t reimplement — canonical C/C++/Fortran reference code wrapped via Rcpp wherever solid reference implementation exists, rather re-derived pure R.

title: Rtractor — Complexity and nonlinear time series analysis for R
text: 🌙 mrpheus — raw PSG/EEG signal analysis ⚡ zeitR — wrist actigraphy pipeline 🔄 hypnoR — staging-agnostic hypnogram analysis 🌀 dynR — dynamic functional connectivity 🔬 circadia-bio — Circadia Lab GitHub organisation

title: Rtractor — Complexity and nonlinear time series analysis for R
text: Released GNU General Public License, version 2 later (GPL (>= 2)). wrapped reference implementations GPL-licensed — see inst/COPYRIGHTS attribution citation requirements. Copyright © Circadia Lab, 2026

what: Rtractor: Complexity and Nonlinear Time Series Analysis for Physiological Signals — Rtractor-package
title: Rtractor: Complexity and Nonlinear Time Series Analysis for Physiological Signals — Rtractor-package
text: staging- signal-agnostic toolkit complex systems measures physiological time series: entropy (sample, permutation, approximate), fractal multifractal analysis (Higuchi dimension, DFA, MFDFA), Lyapunov exponents, multiscale entropy, recurrence quantification analysis. Core algorithms wrap established C/C++/Fortran reference implementations rather reimplementing pure R, preserve numerical parity original methods literature. wrapped reference implementations GPL-licensed (see inst/COPYRIGHTS), package whole licensed GPL (>= 2) rather MIT. Designed sit underneath Circadia Lab CoDe-Neuro Lab packages (mrpheus, hypnoR, zeitR, dynR) shared complexity-metrics layer, remaining fully usable standalone numeric time series.

title: Rtractor: Complexity and Nonlinear Time Series Analysis for Physiological Signals — Rtractor-package
text: Maintainer: Lucas França lucas.franca@northumbria.ac.uk (ORCID) Authors: Lucas França lucas.franca@northumbria.ac.uk (ORCID) Mario Leocadio-Miguel mario.miguel@northumbria.ac.uk (ORCID)

what: Multifractal Spectrum via the Chhabra-Jensen Method — chhabra_jensen
title: Multifractal Spectrum via the Chhabra-Jensen Method — chhabra_jensen
text: Estimates multifractal spectrum f(alpha) generalised dimension spectrum D(q) strictly positive time series using direct box-counting method Chhabra & Jensen (1989). Clean-room C++ reimplementation published algorithm (reference MATLAB implementation consulted, ChhabraJensen_Yuj_w0.m, co-authored L. Franca, license header; see inst/COPYRIGHTS). moments core validated Python transliteration reference synthetic test data (exact match displayed precision).

title: Multifractal Spectrum via the Chhabra-Jensen Method — chhabra_jensen
code: chhabra_jensen(   x,   q_values = c(seq(-10, -0.1, by = 0.1), seq(1, 10, by = 0.1)),   scales = NULL )

title: Multifractal Spectrum via the Chhabra-Jensen Method — chhabra_jensen
text: x Numeric vector, strictly positive (treat measure — e.g. apply sigmoid transform first data can negative). length(x) must evenly divisible 2^scale every value scales, .e. dyadic lengths (powers two) work best. q_values Numeric vector multifractal orders. Per original author's guidance, values strictly 0 1 (exclusive) numerically unstable method best avoided – warning issued supplied. Default skips range: c(seq(-10, -0.1, = 0.1), seq(1, 10, = 0.1)). scales Integer vector box-counting scale exponents; box size scale 2^scale. Default 1:floor(log2(length(x)) - 2), keeps least 4 points per box coarsest scale.

title: Multifractal Spectrum via the Chhabra-Jensen Method — chhabra_jensen
text: list : alpha Singularity strength alpha(q). falpha Multifractal spectrum f(alpha(q)). Dq Generalised dimension spectrum D(q). r_squared_alpha, r_squared_falpha, r_squared_Dq R-squared linear fit underlying , per q – inspect trusting given q value's estimate. q q_values used. mu_scale, Ma, Mf, Md underlying regression inputs, included completeness.

title: Multifractal Spectrum via the Chhabra-Jensen Method — chhabra_jensen
text: Chhabra , Jensen RV. Direct determination f(alpha) singularity spectrum. Phys Rev Lett 1989;62:1327-1330.

title: Multifractal Spectrum via the Chhabra-Jensen Method — chhabra_jensen
code: set.seed(1) x <- abs(rnorm(1024)) + 0.01 res <- chhabra_jensen(x, scales = 1:6) plot(res$alpha, res$falpha, type = "b")

title: Detrended Fluctuation Analysis (DFA) — dfa
text: Estimates scaling exponent alpha time series using Detrended Fluctuation Analysis (Peng et al. 1994). direct C++ port reference dfa.c implementation distributed PhysioNet (Mietus, Peng & Moody), validated reproduce original compiled binary's output exactly synthetic test data — see inst/COPYRIGHTS.

title: Detrended Fluctuation Analysis (DFA) — dfa
code: dfa(   x,   order = 1,   min_box = NULL,   max_box = NULL,   integrate = TRUE,   sliding_window = FALSE )

title: Detrended Fluctuation Analysis (DFA) — dfa
text: x Numeric vector. time series analyse. order Integer >= 1. Order polynomial detrending fit (1 = linear detrending, DFA default; 2 = quadratic, etc.). Corresponds nfit - 1 original dfa.c. min_box, max_box Integer NULL. Smallest/largest box size evaluate. Defaults (original): min_box = 2 * (order + 1), max_box = length(x) / 4. integrate Logical. TRUE (default), x treated increment series cumulatively summed analysis (usual DFA convention — e.g. pass RR-interval deviations, cumulative profile). Set FALSE x already integrated/cumulative profile. sliding_window Logical. Use overlapping (sliding) windows instead non-overlapping boxes. Default FALSE.

title: Detrended Fluctuation Analysis (DFA) — dfa
text: list : n Box sizes evaluated (integer vector). F RMS fluctuation box size (numeric vector). alpha DFA scaling exponent: slope log10(F) log10(n).

title: Detrended Fluctuation Analysis (DFA) — dfa
text: Peng C-K, Buldyrev SV, Havlin S, Simons M, Stanley , Goldberger AL. Mosaic organization DNA nucleotides. Phys Rev E 1994;49:1685-1689.

what: Build a palette function from an Rtractor palette — .rtractor_pal
title: Build a palette function from an Rtractor palette — .rtractor_pal
text: Returns function interpolates n colours named palette. Used internally scale constructors.

title: Build a palette function from an Rtractor palette — .rtractor_pal
Build a palette function from an Rtractor palette — .rtractor_pal
text: palette Palette name passed rtractor_palette(). reverse Logical. Reverse palette?

title: Rtractor colour palette — .rtractor_palettes
text: Named list palettes available Rtractor package.

title: Rtractor colour palette — .rtractor_palettes
text: named list character vectors, containing hex colour codes.

title: Higuchi Fractal Dimension — higuchi_fd
text: Estimates fractal dimension time series using Higuchi's (1988) curve-length algorithm. clean-room C++ reimplementation, validated MATLAB reference implementation synthetic test data — see inst/COPYRIGHTS.

title: Higuchi Fractal Dimension — higuchi_fd
text: x Numeric vector. time series analyse. k_max Integer (>= 2). Maximum sub-series interval k. Choose based log-log curve plateaus signal (see Doyle et al. guidance postural-sway / physiological contexts).

title: Higuchi Fractal Dimension — higuchi_fd
text: list : k k values evaluated, 1:k_max. L Average curve length k. hfd Higuchi Fractal Dimension: negative slope log(L) log(1/k).

title: Higuchi Fractal Dimension — higuchi_fd
text: Higuchi T. Approach irregular time series basis fractal theory. Physica D 1988;31(2):277-283.

title: Hjorth Mobility and Complexity — hjorth_parameters
text: Computes Hjorth parameters (Hjorth 1970): mobility, proxy mean frequency derived variance ratio first difference signal ; complexity, proxy bandwidth/irregularity derived second difference. Ported Lucas Franca's mrpheus package (AASM staging feature pipeline). Uses Bessel-corrected (ddof = 1) variance throughout, deliberately matching R's var() convention – mrpheus's original design choice, differs slightly antropy Python library's population-variance (ddof = 0) convention. two converge length(x) grows differ meaningfully short series; see inst/COPYRIGHTS.

title: Hjorth Mobility and Complexity — hjorth_parameters
text: x Numeric vector. time series analyse.

title: Hjorth Mobility and Complexity — hjorth_parameters
text: named list mobility complexity.

title: Hjorth Mobility and Complexity — hjorth_parameters
text: Hjorth B. EEG analysis based time domain properties. Electroencephalogr Clin Neurophysiol 1970;29(3):306-310.

title: Multifractal Detrending Moving Average (MFDMA) — mfdma
text: Estimates multifractal scaling properties time series using detrending moving average algorithm (Gu & Zhou 2010). Clean-room C++ reimplementation published algorithm (reference MATLAB implementation consulted, MFDMA_1D.m, license header; see inst/COPYRIGHTS). segment-fluctuation core validated Python transliteration reference synthetic test data (exact match displayed precision).

title: Multifractal Detrending Moving Average (MFDMA) — mfdma
code: mfdma(   x,   n_min = 10L,   n_max = NULL,   n_scales = 30L,   theta = 0,   q = seq(-4, 4, by = 0.1) )

title: Multifractal Detrending Moving Average (MFDMA) — mfdma
text: x Numeric vector. time series analyse. n_min, n_max Integer. Lower/upper bound segment size n. Following reference implementation's guidance: n_min around 10; n_max around 10% length(x). Defaults: n_min = 10, n_max = round(length(x) / 10). n_scales Integer. Number segment sizes evaluate (log-spaced n_min n_max). Default 30. theta Numeric [0, 1]. Position moving-average window: 0 (default, recommended) = backward MFDMA, 0.5 = centered, 1 = forward. q Numeric vector. Multifractal orders evaluate. Default seq(-4, 4, = 0.1).

title: Multifractal Detrending Moving Average (MFDMA) — mfdma
text: list : n Segment sizes evaluated. Fq Matrix q-th order fluctuation function (segment size x q). tau Multifractal scaling exponent tau(q). alpha Singularity strength alpha(q) (trimmed ends local-slope smoothing window; shorter q). f Multifractal spectrum f(alpha). q q values corresponding alpha/f (trimmed match).

title: Multifractal Detrending Moving Average (MFDMA) — mfdma
text: Gu GF, Zhou WX. Detrending moving average algorithm multifractals. Phys Rev E 2010;82:011136.

title: Multifractal Detrending Moving Average (MFDMA) — mfdma
code: set.seed(1) x <- rnorm(4000) res <- mfdma(x, n_min = 10, n_max = 400, n_scales = 20) plot(res$alpha, res$f, type = "b")

title: Number of Zero Crossings — num_zerocross
text: Counts sign changes time series – simple time-domain proxy dominant frequency / signal roughness, commonly reported alongside Hjorth parameters fractal dimension EEG complexity work. Ported Lucas Franca's mrpheus package (AASM staging feature pipeline), validated exact parity antropy Python library; re-validated directly antropy 0.2.2 synthetic test data (exact match). See inst/COPYRIGHTS.

title: Number of Zero Crossings — num_zerocross
text: x Numeric vector. time series analyse.

title: Number of Zero Crossings — num_zerocross
text: length-1 integer: number zero crossings.

title: Permutation Entropy — perm_entropy
text: Estimates complexity time series using permutation entropy (Bandt & Pompe 2002): Shannon entropy distribution ordinal patterns ("motifs") length order found series. Ported Lucas Franca's mrpheus package (part AASM sleep-staging feature pipeline), validated exact parity antropy Python library; re-validated directly antropy 0.2.2 synthetic test data (exact match displayed precision). See inst/COPYRIGHTS.

title: Permutation Entropy — perm_entropy
text: x Numeric vector. time series analyse. order Integer >= 2. Length ordinal pattern (embedding dimension). Default 3. delay Integer >= 1. Time delay pattern elements. Default 1. normalize Logical. TRUE (default), divide log(order!) result falls [0, 1]. FALSE, return raw Shannon entropy nats (natural log). Note differs antropy Python library, computes raw value bits (log base 2); normalized value base-independent matches antropy exactly, raw values directly comparable two. See inst/COPYRIGHTS.

title: Permutation Entropy — perm_entropy
text: length-1 numeric: permutation entropy.

title: Permutation Entropy — perm_entropy
text: Bandt C, Pompe B. Permutation entropy: natural complexity measure time series. Phys Rev Lett 2002;88:174102.

title: Petrosian Fractal Dimension — petrosian_fd
text: Estimates fractal dimension rate sign changes time series' first difference (fast proxy signal irregularity). Ported Lucas Franca's mrpheus package (AASM staging feature pipeline), validated exact parity antropy Python library; re-validated directly antropy 0.2.2 synthetic test data (exact match). See inst/COPYRIGHTS.

title: Petrosian Fractal Dimension — petrosian_fd
text: x Numeric vector. time series analyse.

title: Petrosian Fractal Dimension — petrosian_fd
text: length-1 numeric: Petrosian fractal dimension.

title: Petrosian Fractal Dimension — petrosian_fd
text: Petrosian . Kolmogorov complexity finite sequences recognition different preictal EEG patterns. Proceedings Eighth IEEE Symposium Computer-Based Medical Systems 1995:212-217.

what: Recurrence Microstates Entropy (maximum-entropy threshold search) — recurrence_microstate_entropy
title: Recurrence Microstates Entropy (maximum-entropy threshold search) — recurrence_microstate_entropy
text: Estimates complexity (windowed) time series using entropy recurrence microstates (Corso et al. 2018), searching vicinity threshold epsilon find value maximises Shannon entropy microstate distribution — parameter-free alternative fixing epsilon hand, related optimal-threshold method Prado et al. (2018).

title: Recurrence Microstates Entropy (maximum-entropy threshold search) — recurrence_microstate_entropy
code: recurrence_microstate_entropy(   x,   block = 3L,   n_samples = 18000L,   eps_min = 1e-09,   eps_max = 0.499999999,   frac = 10L,   frac2 = 10L,   seed = NULL )

title: Recurrence Microstates Entropy (maximum-entropy threshold search) — recurrence_microstate_entropy
text: x Numeric vector. (already windowed) time series segment analyse. original study, x min-max normalised [0, 1] per window calling function — want comparable eps_max values across windows/subjects. block Integer. Side length square recurrence microstate block; microstate space 2^(block^2) possible patterns. Default 3 (matching reference implementation). n_samples Integer. Number random point-pairs sampled estimate recurrence microstate distribution. Default 18000. eps_min, eps_max Numeric. Search range vicinity threshold. Defaults match reference implementation. frac, frac2 Integer. Number steps coarse search pass number refinement iterations, respectively. Defaults match reference implementation. seed Optional integer. Seeds random pair sampling reproducibility. original Julia script reseeds system entropy every run (Random.seed!() argument) therefore reproducible run--run design; pass seed need reproducible results, leave NULL match original's fresh-randomness-every-call behaviour.

title: Recurrence Microstates Entropy (maximum-entropy threshold search) — recurrence_microstate_entropy
text: list : microstate_probs Probability 2^(block^2) microstates entropy-maximising threshold. entropy_max maximum Shannon entropy found (S_max). eps_max vicinity threshold entropy_max achieved.

title: Recurrence Microstates Entropy (maximum-entropy threshold search) — recurrence_microstate_entropy
text: Ported circadia-bio/maxEntropy (Entropy.jl, MIT licensed), script used produce entropy estimates Ferre et al. (2024, PLOS ONE). deterministic core loop validated Python transliteration original synthetic test data (exact match S_max eps_max given identical sampled index pairs). reusable Max_Entropy() routine ported — original script's main() (file /O named subject directories) study-specific scaffolding, part general-purpose method.

title: Recurrence Microstates Entropy (maximum-entropy threshold search) — recurrence_microstate_entropy
text: Corso G, Prado TL, dos Santos Lima GZ, Kurths J, Lopes SR. Quantifying entropy using recurrence matrix microstates. Chaos 2018;28(8):083108. Prado TL, dos Santos Lima GZ, Lobao-Soares B, Nascimento GC, Corso G, Fontenele-Araujo J, Kurths J, Lopes SR. Optimizing detection nonstationary signals using recurrence analysis. Chaos 2018;28(8):085703. Ferre IBS, Corso G, dos Santos Lima GZ, Lopes SR, Leocadio-Miguel MA, Franca LGS, de Lima Prado T, Araujo JF. Cycling reduces entropy neuronal activity human adult cortex. PLOS ONE 2024;19(10):e0298703.

title: Recurrence Microstates Entropy (maximum-entropy threshold search) — recurrence_microstate_entropy
Retrieve an Rtractor palette — rtractor_palette
text: Returns named character vector hex colour codes requested palette. Suitable direct use ggplot2::scale_fill_manual() ggplot2::scale_colour_manual().

title: Retrieve an Rtractor palette — rtractor_palette
text: palette Name palette. One "main", "core", "diverging", "seq_blue", "seq_sage", "seq_coral". Defaults "main". n Number colours return. NULL (default) colours palette returned. n smaller palette length first n colours returned; larger error thrown. reverse Logical. Reverse order palette? Default FALSE.

title: Retrieve an Rtractor palette — rtractor_palette
text: named (unnamed gradient palettes) character vector hex colour codes.

title: Retrieve an Rtractor palette — rtractor_palette
code: rtractor_palette() #>      coral      cream       sage steel_blue        ink  #>  "#FFB6A6"  "#FFEBD3"  "#9BCEC1"  "#67A2C5"  "#23475C"  rtractor_palette("core") #>      coral      cream       sage steel_blue  #>  "#FFB6A6"  "#FFEBD3"  "#9BCEC1"  "#67A2C5"  rtractor_palette("seq_blue", n = 3) #> [1] "#D9E8F0" "#9DC1D6" "#67A2C5" rtractor_palette("diverging", reverse = TRUE) #> [1] "#67A2C5" "#9BCEC1" "#CCE3D3" "#FFEBD3" "#FFD3BE" "#FFB6A6"

what: List all available Rtractor palettes — rtractor_palettes
title: List all available Rtractor palettes — rtractor_palettes
text: Prints names sizes palettes defined package.

title: List all available Rtractor palettes — rtractor_palettes
List all available Rtractor palettes — rtractor_palettes
text: named integer vector palette lengths, invisibly.

title: List all available Rtractor palettes — rtractor_palettes
code: rtractor_palettes() #> Available Rtractor palettes: #>   Qualitative: #>     main                  5 colours #>     core                  4 colours #>   Diverging: #>     diverging             6 colours #>   Sequential: #>     seq_blue              5 colours #>     seq_sage              5 colours #>     seq_coral             5 colours

title: Rtractor discrete colour scale — scale_colour_rtractor
text: Applies Rtractor qualitative palette colour aesthetic. Defaults "core" (excludes dark ink colour, reserved text/axis annotation rather data encoding).

title: Rtractor discrete colour scale — scale_colour_rtractor
code: scale_colour_rtractor(palette = "core", reverse = FALSE, ...)  scale_color_rtractor(palette = "core", reverse = FALSE, ...)

title: Rtractor discrete colour scale — scale_colour_rtractor
text: palette Palette name. Default "core". reverse Logical. Reverse palette? Default FALSE. ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::discrete_scale().

title: Rtractor discrete colour scale — scale_colour_rtractor
code: library(ggplot2) ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, colour = factor(cyl))) +   geom_point(size = 3) +   scale_colour_rtractor() +   theme_rtractor()

title: Rtractor continuous colour scale — scale_colour_rtractor_c
text: Interpolates across Rtractor palette continuous colour data. "diverging" palette recommended signed quantities (e.g. Lyapunov exponent sign, spectrum asymmetry); seq_* palettes unipolar data (e.g. entropy magnitude).

title: Rtractor continuous colour scale — scale_colour_rtractor_c
code: scale_colour_rtractor_c(palette = "seq_blue", reverse = FALSE, ...)  scale_color_rtractor_c(palette = "seq_blue", reverse = FALSE, ...)

title: Rtractor continuous colour scale — scale_colour_rtractor_c
text: palette Palette name. Default "seq_blue". reverse Logical. Reverse palette? Default FALSE. ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::scale_colour_gradientn().

title: Rtractor discrete fill scale — scale_fill_rtractor
text: Applies Rtractor qualitative palette fill aesthetic.

title: Rtractor discrete fill scale — scale_fill_rtractor
text: palette Palette name. Default "core". reverse Logical. Reverse palette? Default FALSE. ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::discrete_scale().

title: Rtractor discrete fill scale — scale_fill_rtractor
code: library(ggplot2) ggplot(mpg, aes(class, fill = drv)) +   geom_bar() +   scale_fill_rtractor() +   theme_rtractor()

title: Rtractor continuous fill scale — scale_fill_rtractor_c
text: Interpolates across Rtractor palette continuous fill data.

title: Rtractor continuous fill scale — scale_fill_rtractor_c
text: palette Palette name. Default "seq_blue". reverse Logical. Reverse palette? Default FALSE. ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::scale_colour_gradientn().

title: Rtractor ggplot2 theme — theme_rtractor
text: clean ggplot2 theme built ggplot2::theme_minimal(), visually distinct theme_circadia() (softer palette, structural conventions) Rtractor figures recognisable glance.

title: Rtractor ggplot2 theme — theme_rtractor
text: base_size Base font size points. Default 14. base_family Base font family. Default "" (ggplot2 default). grid grid lines show. One "none" (default), "y" (horizontal ), "xy" (), "x" (vertical ). legend_position Position legend passed ggplot2::theme(). Default "right".

title: Rtractor ggplot2 theme — theme_rtractor
text: ggplot2::theme() object.

title: Rtractor ggplot2 theme — theme_rtractor
code: library(ggplot2) ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, colour = factor(cyl))) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   scale_colour_rtractor() +   theme_rtractor()

title: Rtractor 0.0.0.9000
text: Added perm_entropy(), petrosian_fd(), hjorth_parameters(), num_zerocross() — four nonlinear/complexity features centralised Lucas França’s mrpheus package (AASM staging feature pipeline, validated R/C++ port YASA/antropy Python feature set). Re-validated directly antropy 0.2.2 port: exact match perm_entropy (normalized value), petrosian_fd, num_zerocross. hjorth_parameters agrees closely exactly design — uses Bessel-corrected (ddof=1) variance, matching mrpheus’s/R’s convention, rather antropy’s population-variance (ddof=0) convention; difference shrinks signal length grows. Rtractor’s existing higuchi_fd() cross-checked mrpheus/antropy’s version found agree exactly realistic epoch lengths.

title: Rtractor 0.0.0.9000
text: Added dfa() — Detrended Fluctuation Analysis. Direct C++ port PhysioNet’s reference dfa.c (Mietus, Peng & Moody), validated reproduce compiled reference binary’s output exactly synthetic test data. Package license changed GPL (>= 2) result (see inst/COPYRIGHTS). Added higuchi_fd() — Higuchi Fractal Dimension. Clean-room C++ reimplementation Higuchi (1988), validated MATLAB reference implementation synthetic test data (max abs. difference ~1e-11). Added mfdma() — multifractal detrending moving average (Gu & Zhou 2010). Clean-room C++ reimplementation published algorithm, segment-fluctuation core validated Python transliteration reference MATLAB implementation synthetic test data (exact match displayed precision). Added chhabra_jensen() — multifractal spectrum via Chhabra-Jensen box-counting method (1989). Clean-room C++ reimplementation, moments core validated Python transliteration reference implementation (exact match displayed precision).

title: Rtractor 0.0.0.9000
text: Added recurrence_microstate_entropy() — recurrence microstates maximum-entropy threshold search (Corso et al. 2018; Prado et al. 2018). Direct C++ port deterministic core Max_Entropy() circadia-bio/maxEntropy (Entropy.jl, MIT licensed), script used Ferre et al. (2024, PLOS ONE). Random pair sampling moved R set.seed()-reproducibility; core loop validated Python transliteration original (exact match synthetic test data).

title: Rtractor 0.0.0.9000
text: Repository initialised: package skeleton (DESCRIPTION, .Rprofile, R/, src/, tests/testthat/) exported functions yet. Added Rtractor colour palette (rtractor_palette(), rtractor_palettes()), discrete/continuous ggplot2 scales (scale_colour_rtractor(), scale_fill_rtractor(), scale_colour_rtractor_c(), scale_fill_rtractor_c()), theme_rtractor(). Planned function families stubbed file headers (implementations yet, pending reference code inventory): entropy, fractal, lyapunov, multiscale, rqa, embed.
